scalar.description: A scalar variable holds a single value - string, number, or reference. Prefixed with $.

array.description: An array holds an ordered list of scalars. Prefixed with @. Access elements with $array[index].

hash.description: A hash holds key-value pairs. Prefixed with %. Access values with $hash{key}.

sub.description: Defines a subroutine (function). Arguments are passed via @_.

my.description: Declares a lexically scoped variable. Always use with 'use strict'.

use.description: Load and import a module at compile time.

regex.description: Perl regex matching. Flags: g (global), i (case-insensitive), m (multiline), s (single-line), x (extended).

substitution.description: Regex substitution. Replaces matched text with replacement string.

chomp.description: Removes the trailing newline from a string. Returns number of characters removed.

split.description: Split a string into a list based on a delimiter pattern.

join.description: Join list elements into a single string with separator.

print.description: Output to stdout or a filehandle. No newline added automatically.

die.description: Raise an exception. Commonly used with or: open() or die "...";

strict.description: Enables strict mode - requires variable declaration, restricts barewords, and prevents soft references.

warnings.description: Enables compile-time and runtime warnings for common mistakes.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.
